title: Shef Hours Log — Dec 2025 / Jan 2026
html:
TL;DR

  • Apple Note from 2026-01-08 with logged shifts at Shef across late December 2025 and January 2026: 12/15 (5h), 12/16 (4h), then later additions for 1/11 and 1/12.
  • First in-corpus quantitative datapoint on Yahir's Shef gig — a 9-hour first-pay-period pace, suggesting it was a low-commitment side hustle slotted around Rankett / Swipr focus blocks.
  • Reinforces the bridge income stack thread: Yahir maintaining 4-6 income micro-streams in early 2026 (Shef, gym overnight shift, NaviWell, Rankett client work).
  • The "1/11/2025" / "1/12/2025" entries are date typos — Yahir wrote 2025 when he meant 2026 (a common Jan-of-new-year mistake), but reinforces this is a hand-thumbed Apple Notes log with zero auto-tracking.
  • Pure time tracking as pay stub note: this is the timesheet Yahir submits to Shef for hours pay.

Notes

The log, in source order:

  • 12pm – 5pm (12/15/2025) — 5 hours
  • 8am – 12pm (12/16/2025) — 4 hours
  • "9 Total Hours"
  • 1/11/2025 [sic — 2026]: 8am – 4:30pm (~8.5 hours)
  • 1/12/2025 [sic — 2026]: 9:30 – 2:30 (5 hours)

The first weekend (Dec 15-16) was a tidy 9-hour test run — likely the Shef onboarding / first weekend on the freezer-stocking-and-pickups runbook captured in the Shef instruction note from four days earlier. The second pair (Jan 11-12) is heavier (~13.5 hours combined), suggesting Yahir had ramped to a regular weekend shift by that point.

The note's structure — start time, end time, total hours under a date — is a pay-stub-shaped artifact. Yahir is keeping his own timesheet because Shef's marketplace probably tracks pickups / drop-offs but not hands-on freezer time. This is the same pattern as informal hourly gig workers across DoorDash / Instacart / pet-sitting marketplaces.

In the bridge income taxonomy, Shef is the physical leg (showing up to a kitchen, moving cold food); Rankett client work is the cognitive leg; the gym overnight shift is the time-arbitrage leg. This note is one of the only quantitative looks at the dollar/hour ratio Yahir was actually achieving in early 2026.
